summary:
To address edge-to-edge contact between complete denture teeth in centric occlusion, it is recommended to adjust cusp positions gently to balance occlusal forces effectively without drastically altering tooth morphology or causing undue tissue strain; this ensures proper guidance of mandibular motion and enhances overall function such as speaking and chewing efficiently.
The primary goal involves refining specific areas through careful analysis and targeted modifications aimed at distributing force more equitably across all surfaces involved, thereby promoting long-term health benefits over just addressing immediate discomfort issues presented initially when considering full-scale reconstruction approaches might not always align best interests fully given individual anatomical variations present among patients seeking dental prostheses care services today.
